description.en:
## Overview

Singha Park is a massive 8,000-rai agricultural tourism estate operated by Boon Rawd Brewery, the company behind Thailand's iconic Singha beer. Located about 10 kilometers west of Chiang Rai city off Route 1211, the park spreads across rolling hills covered with Oolong tea plantations, fruit orchards, flower gardens, and grazing pastures. Admission to the park grounds is free, making it one of the best value attractions in northern Thailand, though individual activities carry small fees.

## Activities and Attractions

The park offers a wide range of activities for all ages. The Singha Farm Tour by open-air tram costs just 50 THB per person and loops through the tea plantations and fruit orchards with stops at scenic viewpoints. Mountain bike rental is available for 150 THB per hour for adults and 50 THB for children, letting you explore the park's extensive network of cycling trails at your own pace. For thrill-seekers, the zipline course costs 300 THB and offers an aerial view of the tea fields. The park also features a small zoo with African animals including giraffes and zebras, a swan lake, and seasonal flower fields that are especially photogenic from November through February.

## Food and Drink

Singha Park takes its agricultural products seriously. The on-site Bhu Bhirom restaurant, open until 10:00 PM, serves dishes made from ingredients grown on the farm paired with Singha beer and locally produced tea. Throughout the park you will find smaller cafes and food stalls serving fresh fruit smoothies, Oolong tea lattes, and snacks. The park's own tea brand produces high-quality Oolong that makes an excellent souvenir.

## Practical Information

Singha Park is open daily from 9:00 AM to 6:00 PM, with some restaurants staying open until 10:00 or 11:00 PM. The park is free to enter; you only pay for specific activities. The cool season from November to February is the best time to visit when the flower fields are in bloom and temperatures are comfortable for cycling. The park is large enough that you could easily spend half a day here, especially if combining the farm tour, cycling, and a meal.
description.nl: Groot landbouwpark met theeplantages ballonvaarten en buitenactiviteiten

entry_fee: Free admission; Farm Tour tram 50 THB, bike rental 150 THB/hour, zipline 300 THB
best_time_to_visit: November to February when flower fields bloom and temperatures are comfortable for cycling
tips: The 50 THB farm tour tram is excellent value and covers the highlights if you are short on time
Bring your own water bottle as the park is massive and walking between areas takes time
Stay for dinner at Bhu Bhirom restaurant which is open until 10 PM with farm-to-table dishes and Singha beer
